label: true-dup
a: Created a compound index on events(tenant_id, created_at DESC). The slow queries were all in the pattern SELECT * FROM events WHERE tenant_id = $1 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 100. The compound index satisfies both the filter and the sort without a separate sort step.
b: Added index idx_events_tenant_time on events(tenant_id, created_at DESC). This was the missing index for the tenant-scoped time-ordered event fetches. Before: 4.2 seconds (full table scan + sort on 40M rows). After: 18 ms (index-only scan).
reviewer_notes: Both describe the same compound index events(tenant_id, created_at DESC) for the same slow-query pattern.
